__CheckParams.summary: Return public members of a type with a specific name (case insensitive)

__FoxArray.summary:
Internal type that implements the FoxPro Compatible ARRAY type.
This type has methods and properties that normally are never directly called from user code.
__FoxArray.remarks:
FoxPro arrays can be one dimensional or two dimensional. Two dimensional arrays are implemented as a projection on top of a one dimensional array.
So a two dimensional array of 4 x 3 elements will be implemented as an array of 12 elements. The elements 1-3 represent row 1, 4-6 row 2 etc.

__FoxArray.DeleteColumn.summary: Delete a column from a multi dimensional array.
__FoxArray.DeleteColumn.remarks:
When no columns are defined then nothing happens.
When an array has columns then the elements are deleted and shifted.
Assume a 4 x 4 array. When DeleteColumn() is called with column 2 then the elements 2,6,10 and 14 are deleted and the elements 3 & 4, 7 & 8, 11 & 12 and 15 & 16 are shifted to the left. New empty elements are inserted on locations 4,8,12 and 16.

__FoxArray.DeleteRow.summary: Delete a row from a multi dimensional array.
__FoxArray.DeleteRow.remarks: Deleting rows will shift other rows up and will add a blank row at the bottom of the array

__FoxArray.InsertColumn.summary: Insert a column into a multi dimensional array.
__FoxArray.InsertColumn.remarks: Inserting columns deletes columns at the end of each row.

__FoxArray.InsertRow.summary: Insert a row into a multi dimensional array.
__FoxArray.InsertRow.remarks: Inserting rows shifts rows up and deletes a row at the end of the array: FoxPro arrays do not grow automatically

__FoxArray.SELF_index.returns: The value of the property of the element stored at the indicated location in the array.

__FoxArrayAccess.summary: Access an element in a FoxPro array with 2 dimensions

__FoxArrayAccess_2.summary: Access an element in a FoxPro array with 1 dimension

__FoxAssign.summary: This function gets called for assignments in FoxPro, so aFoo := 42 will will the complete array when aFoo is an array

__FoxGetWithExpression.summary: Retrieve an element from the FoxPro With stack

__FoxMemVarPut.summary: This function replaces the normal MemVarPut to make sure that arrays are filled when they are assigned

__FoxPopWithBlock.summary: Pop element from the FoxPro With stack

__FoxPushWithBlock.summary: Push element to the FoxPro With stack

__GetFoxWithStack.summary: Retrieve the With Stack for the FoxPro dialect (so you can call methods or properties outside of the scope of the WITH block)

__IVarGetOrSend.summary: Call a method or retrieve an indexed property from an object.

__IVarGetOrSend_2.summary: Call a method or retrieve an indexed property from an object.

_ASCIICOLS.summary: Specifies the number of columns in an ASCII text file created with the ASCII keyword in the REPORT FORM command.

_ASCIIROWS.summary: Specifies the number of rows in an ASCII text file created with the ASCII keyword in the REPORT FORM command.

_MLINE.summary: Contains the memo field offset for the MLINE( ) function.

_PAGETOTAL.summary: Contains the total number of pages in a report.

_PRETEXT.summary: Specifies a character expression to preface text merge lines.

_TALLY.summary: Contains the number of records processed by the most recently executed table command.

_TEXT.summary: File handle for the output file for the TEXT .. ENDTEXT and \ and \\ commands.

_TRIGGERLEVEL.summary: Contains a numeric value indicating the current trigger procedure nesting level.

ADel.remarks: The parameter to this function is a 'General Array'. The function decides at runtime if the array is a FoxPro array or a 'General' Array

AIns.remarks: The parameter to this function is a 'General Array'. The function decides at runtime if the array is a FoxPro array or a 'General' Array

CreateObject.summary: Creates an object from a class definition or an Automation-enabled application.
CreateObject.param: Specifies the class or OLE object from which the new object is created.
These optional parameters are used to pass values to the Init event procedure for the class. The Init event is executed when you issue CREATEOBJECT( ) and allows you to initialize the object.

DialectChanged.summary: This event handler is attached to the DialectChanged event of the RuntimeState, to initialize the FoxPro state

FoxCollations.summary: List of possible index collations for VFP DBF files. For each of these collations there is a weight table in the XSharp.VFP assembly.

FoxCollations.CZECH.summary: CZech collation. Supported are codepages 852, 895 and 1250

FoxCollations.DUTCH.summary: Dutch collation. Supported are codepages 437, 850 and 1252

FoxCollations.GENERAL.summary: General collation. Supported are codepages 437, 620, 850,852,857,861,865,895,1250,1252 and 1254

FoxCollations.GERMAN.summary:
German phone book order (DIN) collation. Supported are codepages 437, and 1252
This collation is used for English, French, German, Modern Spanish, Portuguese, and other Western European languages

FoxCollations.GREEK.summary: Greek collation. Supported are codepages 737, and 1253

FoxCollations.HUNGARY.summary: Hungarian collation. Supported are codepages 852, and 1250

FoxCollations.ICELAND.summary: Icelandic collation. Supported are codepages 437,, 850, 861 and 1252

FoxCollations.MACHINE.summary: Machine collation. Supported are codepages 437, 620, 850,852,857,861,865,895,1250,1251,1252,1253 and 1254

FoxCollations.NORDAN.summary: Norwegian, Danish collation. Supported are codepages 437, 850, 865 and 1252.

FoxCollations.POLISH.summary: Polish collation. Supported are codepages 620, 852 and 1250

FoxCollations.RUSSIAN.summary: Russian collation. Supported are codepages 866 and 1251

FoxCollations.SLOVAK.summary: Slovakian collation. Supported are codepages 852, 895 and 1250

FoxCollations.SPANISH.summary: Traditional Spanish collation. Supported are codepages 437, 850 and 1252

FoxCollations.SWEFIN.summary: Swedish/Finnish collation. Supported are codepages 437, 850, 865 and 1252

FoxCollations.TURKISH.summary: Turkish collation. Supported are codepages 857 and 1254

FoxCollations.UNIQWT.summary: Unique Weight collation. Supported are codepages 437, 850 and 1252

FoxFunctionCategory.summary: Official functional categories of Visual FoxPro

FoxFunctionStatus.summary: FoxPro feature support level in X#

FoxProFunctionAttribute.summary: Attribute to instrument the FoxPro dialect functions vital for generating the compatibility metrics report via Reflection

SetTextFile.summary: Open/Close the file to which TEXT .. ENDTEXT values are written
SetTextFile.param: Name of the file to create. When this name is NULL or Empty then the file will be closed when it is open
SetTextFile.returns: TRUE when the file was succesfully opened / closed.
SetTextFile.remarks: The file handle for the file will be stored in the _TEXT system variable.

SetTextMerge.summary: Retrieve the setting that determines if TextMerge is active

SetTextMerge_2.summary: Change the setting that determines if TextMerge is active
SetTextMerge_2.param: TRUE when Merge must be used for TEXT .. ENDTEXT expressions without TEXTMERGE clause and for the \ and \\ commands.

SqlGetProp.param: Specifies the setting. For a list of the settings you can specify see the SQLProperty Enum.

SqlSetProp.param: Specifies the setting. For a list of the settings you can specify see the SQLProperty Enum.
